title: Pilosella mayeri
taxonomy: Pilosella mayeri is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Pilosella, which consists of several closely related species. The genus Pilosella is part of the tribe Senecioneae, which includes a large number of dandelion-like plants. Pilosella mayeri is native to Europe and is also known as Alpine Hawksbeard. The plant is characterized by its small, daisy-like flowers and hairy leaves.
uses: Pilosella mayeri is used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Its leaves and roots are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. In some regions, the plant is used to make teas and infusions that are said to aid in the treatment of respiratory problems. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Pilosella mayeri for medicinal use. More studies are required to fully understand the plant's potential health benefits.

intro: Pilosella mayeri, also known as a type of dandelion, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The plant's bioactive constituents include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Pilosella mayeri is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to alleviate skin irritations and wounds.
